title: chenopodium polyspermum subsp. spicatum
intro: Chenopodium polyspermum subsp. spicatum, also known as worm-seed or spicate lamb's quarters, is a plant species within the Chenopodium genus, which is part of the Amaranthaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Chenopodium polyspermum subsp. spicatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
